sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Sarcos Robotics Faces Pivotal Earnings Test Following AI Pivot
feed.summary: Palladyne AI Corp. (operating as Sarcos Technology and Robotics) is at a critical juncture, with investors keenly awaiting its upcoming quarterly report to assess the success of its strategic pivot from hardware manufacturing to AI-driven software platforms. The market will focus on financial health indicators and commercial adoption, especially regarding its AI software for robotic systems, to determine if the company can reduce net losses and improve profitability. The timing of the earnings release is uncertain, adding to market caution, yet it is seen as a key catalyst for evaluating the company's new strategic direction amidst a complex market landscape of investor "AI fatigue" and robust demand for autonomous systems, particularly in defense.

feed.title: Rollins at Barclays Conference: Strategic Growth Amid Challenges
feed.summary: Rollins Inc. (NYSE:ROL) discussed its strategic growth plans at the Barclays 43rd Annual Industrial Select Conference, targeting 7-8% organic growth and 2-3% through M&A, despite recent weather-related challenges impacting Q4 performance. The company highlighted its strong recurring revenue model (75%), modernization efforts in financial systems and procurement, and initiatives to improve employee retention and brand collaboration. Rollins also noted the family's continued support for modernization and their long-term commitment to the company's strategic direction.

feed.title: Assurant Earns Feefo’s Platinum Trusted Service Award for 2026 in New Zealand
feed.summary: Assurant, Inc. (NYSE: AIZ) has received the Feefo Platinum Trusted Service Award for 2026 in New Zealand, an exclusive recognition for consistently delivering world-class customer experiences. This award, given to companies achieving the Gold standard for three or more consecutive years, highlights Assurant's sustained commitment to operational excellence and customer trust. The recognition is based on verified customer reviews, with Assurant maintaining a Feefo service rating of 4.5 or higher from a minimum of 50 verified reviews annually since 2023.

feed.title: New Potomac Edison Substation Delivers More Reliable Power for Berkeley County Customers
feed.summary: Potomac Edison, a subsidiary of FirstEnergy Corp., has completed a new $6.6 million substation in Berkeley County, West Virginia, to enhance electric service reliability for approximately 2,400 homes and businesses, particularly in the growing Falling Waters and Spring Mills areas. Completed in December, the facility utilizes modern, smart technology to reduce outages, speed up power restoration, and support the county's continued development. This investment is part of FirstEnergy's broader Energize365 program, which aims to modernize and strengthen the electric grid.

feed.title: Whirlpool Corporation (Amana, Iowa) WARN Act Investigation
feed.summary: Strauss Borrelli PLLC is investigating Whirlpool Corporation regarding a potential mass layoff of 341 employees in Amana, Iowa. The firm believes Whirlpool may have violated the federal WARN Act by failing to provide the required 60-day notice before the layoff. Employees affected by this layoff may be entitled to 60 days of severance pay and benefits.

feed.title: Wingstop shares soar despite same-store sales decline
feed.summary: Wingstop's shares climbed significantly despite the company reporting a decline in same-store domestic sales for the fourth quarter. The CEO anticipates 2025 to be a "transformational year" for the chain, suggesting investor confidence in future growth despite the recent sales dip.

feed.title: CSX: Fair Outlook Weighs New Locomotive Spend Against Rail And Trucking Risks
feed.summary: Analyst price targets for CSX have been adjusted, with some firms raising and others slightly lowering their targets, reflecting varied views on rail volumes, sector risks, and P/E inputs. The company's fair value remains unchanged at $39.85 per share despite minor adjustments to discount rate and future P/E multiple assumptions. Recent news includes a train derailment in Kentucky and a significant deal with Wabtec Corporation to acquire new and modernize existing locomotives.

feed.title: Breaking Down Eastman Chemical: 10 Analysts Share Their Views
feed.summary: Ten analysts have provided varied ratings for Eastman Chemical (EMN) over the past quarter, with an average 12-month price target of $73.50, reflecting a 4.26% increase from the previous average. The company, established in 1920, focuses on higher-margin specialty chemical products, with strong global sales outside the U.S. Financially, Eastman Chemical exhibits a restricted market capitalization, a revenue decline of -12.12% over the last three months, but strong profitability with a 5.32% net margin, 1.79% ROE, 0.7% ROA, and a balanced debt-to-equity ratio of 0.8.

feed.title: Altria Moving Forward with Smoke-Free Products
feed.summary: Altria Group, Inc. reaffirmed its 2026 full-year guidance, projecting adjusted diluted EPS growth of 2.5% to 5.5% from a 2025 base. The company anticipates earnings growth to be weighted towards the second half of the year, driven by increased cigarette import/export activity, pricing power, and capital allocation. Altria also emphasized its strategic shift towards smoke-free products, such as its on! nicotine pouch portfolio, despite challenges in the e-vapor market.

feed.title: Tommy Hilfiger Watches: Are These Movado?Made Timepieces Worth It?
feed.summary: This article reviews Tommy Hilfiger licensed watches, manufactured by Movado Group, to determine their value. It concludes that they are a good choice for fashion-first buyers, gift-givers, and brand fans looking for an accessory under $200, rather than for serious watch enthusiasts seeking high horological value. The watches are praised for their design and branding but noted for using quartz movements and mineral crystals, reflecting their fashion-accessory positioning over technical specifications.

feed.title: Epstein Files Show Royal Caribbean Board Member Tom Pritzker Invited Jeffrey Epstein Aboard Allure of the Seas in 2011
feed.summary: Newly released Epstein files reveal that former Royal Caribbean board member Tom Pritzker invited Jeffrey Epstein aboard the Allure of the Seas in 2011, and that cruise industry mogul Philip Levine also maintained a decades-long relationship with Epstein. The documents detail extensive communications between Epstein and both Pritzker and Levine, even after Epstein's 2008 conviction. One victim alleges she was trafficked to Epstein's private island after being taken off a Royal Caribbean ship.

feed.title: No companies are safe from AI sentiment swings, says Huntington Bank's Marc Dizard
feed.summary: The article discusses the significant impact of AI sentiment swings on various companies, as analyzed by Marc Dizard of Huntington Bank. It implies that no industry or company is immune to how market perceptions and developments in artificial intelligence can affect their valuations and prospects.

feed.title: Richard W. Allen makes significant trades in American Water Works, Paychex, and S&P Global
feed.summary: Richard W. Allen, a Georgia congressman, has engaged in notable stock transactions, selling shares in American Water Works Company, Inc. and Paychex, Inc., and purchasing stocks in S&P Global Inc., along with a US Treasury Note. These moves illustrate a diversified investment strategy across different sectors and asset types. The article highlights American Water Works' consistent dividend payments, noting its 19-year streak and 2.48% yield, as identified by InvestingPro.

feed.title: South End tower nears full occupancy as investment giant inks big lease
feed.summary: Global financial services firm Charles Schwab has signed a lease for nearly 59,000 square feet at 110 East in Charlotte's South End. This significant deal brings the 24-story office tower to 95% occupancy, nearing full capacity. The article highlights the area's continued development and demand for commercial real estate.

feed.title: Intel Turns To Microsoft’s Copilot Studio For Partner Support After Dialing Back Phone Use
feed.summary: Intel has launched "Ask Intel," an AI-based virtual assistant powered by Microsoft's Copilot Studio, to provide customer and partner support. This initiative is part of Intel's broader transition to a "digital-first experience" after restructuring its support operations and scaling back phone and social media support. The virtual assistant aims to improve efficiency, reduce navigation time for support, and allow human agents to focus on more complex issues, with plans for expanded features and languages.

feed.title: Brandywine Realty Trust Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.08 a Share, Payable April 16 to Holders of Record April 2
feed.summary: Brandywine Realty Trust (BDN) has announced that it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.08 per share. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on April 16, 2026, to shareholders who are on record as of April 2, 2026. This announcement follows their recent Q4 2025 earnings call and report, which included earnings results for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2025.

feed.title: Half of burned rail ties moved out of Dunnellon, CSX says
feed.summary: CSX Transportation reports that approximately half of the 100,000 rail ties that burned in a fire in Dunnellon on February 1st have been removed from the site. While CSX owns the property, Track Line LLC owns the ties and had leased the land to Florida Northern. CSX has incurred a daily fine, and though no timeline is provided, efforts are underway to move the remaining ties, with Florida Northern facilitating the move, CSX providing cars, and Track Line covering clean-up costs.

feed.title: Johns Hopkins Dermatology Expands Advanced Care for Inflammatory and Autoimmune Skin Conditions with Addition of XTRAC Excimer Laser
feed.summary: Johns Hopkins Dermatology has integrated the XTRAC® 308 nm excimer laser into its clinical offerings, enhancing its capability to treat chronic inflammatory and autoimmune skin conditions like psoriasis, vitiligo, and atopic dermatitis. This move by Johns Hopkins, a leading academic medical center, underscores its commitment to advanced, targeted patient care and clinical research. STRATA Skin Sciences, the developer of XTRAC, emphasizes its continued partnership with top institutions to advance evidence-based dermatologic innovation and expand access to precision treatment strategies.

feed.title: Allegion plc Is Quietly Rewiring Smart Locks – Here’s Why It Matters
feed.summary: Allegion plc, a major player in security, is discreetly transforming smart access in the US through its brands like Schlage. The company is focusing on smart home integrations, mobile credentials, and AI-ready security platforms for both residential and commercial use. This strategy positions Allegion as a foundational infrastructure provider for secure, flexible access, rather than a flashy gadget manufacturer.

feed.title: Target, Walmart traffic numbers trending in different directions: Placer.ai
feed.summary: Recent data from Placer.ai reveals that Walmart and Target are experiencing contrasting financial trends as their new CEOs take the helm. Walmart, under John Furner, saw an increase in traffic and grocery penetration, while Target, led by Michael Fiddelke, faced declines in foot traffic, especially on weekends. These diverging trends highlight Walmart's strength in value and digital growth versus Target's challenges, prompting the latter to focus on improving assortment and in-store experience.

feed.title: Hedge Fund and Insider Trading News: Bill Ackman, Ray Dalio, David Einhorn, Appaloosa Management, Brevan Howard Asset Management, Maverick Capital, Viking Global, DraftKings Inc (DKNG), Avantor Inc (AVTR), and More
feed.summary: This article compiles recent news in the hedge fund and insider trading world, featuring prominent figures like Bill Ackman, Ray Dalio, and David Einhorn, alongside key firms such as Appaloosa Management and Viking Global. It highlights significant activities including legal challenges for Ackman, Greenlight Capital's increased stakes, Brevan Howard's crypto fund performance, and insider buying in companies like DraftKings and Avantor. The piece also includes a recommendation for a promising AI stock that is trading at less than five times its earnings and has shown positive returns despite recent market declines.

feed.title: Synergy CHC Corp. (NASDAQ: SNYR) Launches FocusFactor
feed.summary: Synergy CHC Corp. (NASDAQ: SNYR) is entering the functional beverage market with a national rollout of FocusFactor Registered Ready-to-Drink Beverage, leveraging its 25-year legacy supplement brand. This strategy allows Synergy to utilize existing retail relationships, including major retailers like Costco and Walmart, reducing customer acquisition costs. The company forecasts this expansion could add $20 million annually to its $35 million revenue, capitalizing on the growing demand for cognitive performance and wellness-focused drinks.

feed.title: Targa Resources Stock Near Record Highs: Smart Buy or Late Chase?
feed.summary: Targa Resources Corp (NYSE: TRGP) has reached near all-time highs due to strong earnings, rising dividends, and bullish Wall Street targets. The company, a major independent midstream infrastructure provider, benefits from growing US natural gas and NGL volumes and surging export demand. While the rally has been significant, investors are now questioning if it remains an attractive entry point or if the easy gains have already been made, emphasizing a focus on long-term value compounding rather than quick re-rating.

feed.title: Union says Whirlpool expands Mexico production as Amana cuts follow last year's layoffs
feed.summary: The International Association of Machinists and Aerospace Workers (IAM) is criticizing Whirlpool Corp.'s decision to lay off nearly 350 union members at its Amana manufacturing facility, viewing it as a pattern of shifting jobs out of the United States. This follows previous layoffs less than a year ago, while Whirlpool simultaneously expands operations in Mexico for refrigerator production, including items exported to the US and Canada. The union calls on local officials to intervene, advocating for American manufacturing and the affected workers.

feed.title: Inside Kroger's thank-you day for more than 400,000 grocery workers
feed.summary: Kroger is celebrating Supermarket Employee Day on February 22, 2026, honoring its more than 400,000 associates as "experience makers" for their service and community connections. The company highlighted specific employees for their dedication and creativity, and invited job seekers to explore career opportunities through its mobile-friendly application process. This initiative underscores Kroger's commitment to its workforce and their impact on customer experience.

feed.title: Bank of America eyes 30 million additional clients with redesign of rewards program
feed.summary: Bank of America is redesigning its rewards program to attract an additional 30 million clients. This initiative involves removing a $20,000 balance requirement, specifically targeting customers who hold personal checking accounts. The Charlotte-based bank aims to replace its former loyalty platform with this new client-focused strategy.

feed.title: Blackboxstocks Inc. (NASDAQ: BLBX) Merger Target, REalloys to Appoint Bob Foresman to its Board of Directors
feed.summary: REalloys Inc., the merger target of Blackboxstocks Inc. (NASDAQ: BLBX), announced the appointment of Bob Foresman to its Board of Directors. Foresman, former Vice Chairman of UBS Investment Bank, will join after the merger closes, bolstering REalloys' financial oversight and strategic growth as it prepares to become a publicly traded company focused on a North American heavy rare earth and defense-focused supply chain. The Form S-4 registration statement has been declared effective, and the merger is expected to close soon, pending NASDAQ listing approval.

feed.title: (AQN) Investment Performance Report (AQN:CA)
feed.summary: This report provides an investment performance update for Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp. (AQN:CA), including AI-generated stock signals and ratings across near, mid, and long terms. It suggests trading plans with specific buy and short targets and stop-loss points. The article also lists numerous recent analytical reports on AQN:CA.

feed.title: Sun Communities, Inc. Announces Distribution Increase
feed.summary: Sun Communities, Inc. (NYSE: SUI) announced that its Board of Directors approved an increase in its quarterly distribution rate to $1.12 per common share and unit, an 8% increase from the prior rate of $1.04. This new rate equates to an annual distribution of $4.48 per common share and is expected to begin with the first-quarter distribution in April 2026. The company is a real estate investment trust specializing in manufactured housing and recreational vehicle communities.

feed.title: The PEPM Death Spiral: How AI Automation is Cannibalizing the Human Capital Management Sector
feed.summary: The Human Capital Management (HCM) software sector is experiencing a "Productivity Paradox" due to AI automation, which is reducing the need for human HR managers and eroding the traditional per-employee-per-month (PEPM) revenue model. This shift has led to stagnating subscriber growth, stock sell-offs, and a pivot towards outcome-based and consumption-based pricing models as companies like Paycom, Workday, and Paylocity adapt to the "AI-first" hiring philosophy. The crisis suggests a broader challenge for the SaaS industry, forcing a re-evaluation of value from headcount to AI-driven outcomes.

feed.title: Federated Hermes Hits All-Time High: What Investors Should Do Next?
feed.summary: Federated Hermes (FHI) recently achieved an all-time high share price, driven by growth in assets under management, strong money market performance, and strategic acquisitions. While the company demonstrates robust financial health, dividend increases, and share repurchase programs, investors are encouraged to remain cautious due to rising operating expenses and revenue concentration. Despite these concerns, FHI's strategic execution, market positioning, and attractive valuation suggest long-term growth potential, leading to a "Hold" rating from Zacks.

feed.title: Is Chipotle Leveraging AI to Reengage Lapsed Customers?
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) is expanding its use of AI within its digital platform to enhance customer engagement, specifically targeting lapsed or less frequent customers with personalized communications and offers. This strategy differentiates Chipotle from competitors like McDonald's, which focuses on operational efficiency, and Yum! Brands, which emphasizes broad digital growth. Despite these efforts, CMG's stock has underperformed, with estimates for fiscal 2026 EPS showing a decline, leading to a Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell).

feed.title: AES Corp.: The Quiet Power Stock Gen Z Investors Are Sleeping On
feed.summary: AES Corp. is presented as a significant, yet overlooked, global power company specializing in clean energy, particularly in the US. The article highlights its role in the clean energy transition through solar, wind, and battery storage projects, along with its consistent dividend payments, making it a "growth-tilted" defensive utility stock. It emphasizes AES's financial stability, long-term contracts, and benefits from US energy trends despite inherent debt and interest rate sensitivities.

feed.title: Brandywine Realty Trust Announces Common Quarterly Dividend, and Confirms First Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call
feed.summary: Brandywine Realty Trust announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.08 per common share and OP Unit, payable on April 16, 2026, to shareholders of record on April 2, 2026. The company also confirmed its plan to release first-quarter 2026 earnings after market close on April 22, 2026, followed by a conference call on April 23, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time. Brandywine Realty Trust is a publicly traded REIT with a focus on real estate in Philadelphia and Austin.

feed.title: AES Corp.: The Quiet Power Stock Gen Z Investors Are Sleeping On
feed.summary: AES Corp. (NYSE: AES) is presented as a significant player in the US clean energy sector, actively transitioning from traditional power generation to renewables like solar, wind, and battery storage. The article highlights its appeal to investors seeking both growth and income due to its dividend payments and long-term contracts. It discusses the company's financial model, focusing on project pipelines, debt management, and its crucial role in supporting increasing energy demands from data centers, EVs, and corporate climate goals.

feed.title: Brandywine Realty Trust Announces Common Quarterly Dividend, and Confirms First Quarter 2026 ...
feed.summary: Brandywine Realty Trust has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.08 per common share and OP Unit, payable on April 16, 2026, to holders of record on April 2, 2026. This dividend is equivalent to an annual rate of $0.32 per share. The company also announced plans to release its first-quarter 2026 earnings after market close on Wednesday, April 22, 2026, and will host a conference call on Thursday, April 23, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time.

feed.title: Global construction prospects 2023
feed.summary: This article provides an outlook on the global construction industry for the year 2023. It aims to offer insights into potential trends, challenges, and opportunities within the sector.

feed.title: Crown Castle (NYSE:CCI) Shares Down 4.6% After Analyst Downgrade
feed.summary: Crown Castle (NYSE:CCI) shares dropped 4.6% after Barclays downgraded its price target from $101 to $91, assigning an equal weight rating. Other firms like Wells Fargo and Jefferies also reduced their targets, leading to a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target price of $100.02. Despite the downgrades, institutional ownership remains high at 90.8%, with recent large purchases by Norges Bank and Lazard.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Stock: Deep Discount Or Sinking Ship?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Royal Caribbean (RCL) stock, exploring whether it presents a deep discount opportunity or is a sinking ship. It highlights RCL's strategic investments, strong booking trends, impressive operating margins, and modest valuation, while also discussing the bear and bull cases regarding potential price wars and capacity increases from competitors. The piece concludes by suggesting alternative stocks and emphasizing the importance of a robust asset allocation framework for wealth management.

feed.title: Contractor sues Gap, alleges HR ignored sexual harassment complaint
feed.summary: Gap, Inc. is facing a federal lawsuit from contractor Michael Ronsini, who alleges sexual harassment, gender discrimination, and retaliation after the company's HR department reportedly failed to act on his complaint. Ronsini claims that after reporting a sexually explicit remark by his supervisor, he faced fabricated performance issues and eventual contract termination, despite years of clean records. The case raises significant questions about how organizations handle harassment complaints from contractors and other non-traditional workers.

feed.title: General Mills (GIS): TD Cowen Lowers Price Target While Holding Rating | GIS Stock News
feed.summary: TD Cowen has lowered its price target for General Mills (GIS) from $47.00 to $45.00, while maintaining a 'Hold' rating on the stock. This adjustment follows similar moves by other analysts, reflecting ongoing evaluations of the company's market performance and future prospects. Despite the lowered target, the average target price from 19 analysts suggests a potential upside of 15.27% from the current price, indicating a "Hold" consensus among brokerage firms.

feed.title: Walmart, Southern Co, Newmont Mining and more set to report earnings Thursday
feed.summary: Thursday marks a significant day for earnings reports, with major companies like Walmart, Southern Co, and Newmont Mining scheduled to release their quarterly results. The article lists estimated EPS and revenue for numerous companies reporting both before and after market close, offering investors a detailed look at upcoming financial disclosures across various sectors.

feed.title: AECOM to leverage TomTom’s location tech
feed.summary: Construction giant AECOM has partnered with mapping and location firm TomTom to integrate TomTom's live traffic data and other location technologies into its infrastructure planning. This collaboration aims to enhance mobility, improve traffic systems management, and increase safety and efficiency on road networks. Despite challenges like federal award activity slowing due to government shutdowns, AECOM anticipates a strong infrastructure cycle for 2026, bolstered by state DOT budgets and forthcoming highway bills.

feed.title: Howmet Aerospace prices $1.2 billion notes offering to fund acquisition By Investing.com
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace (NYSE:HWM) has priced a $1.2 billion public offering of notes to finance its acquisition of Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing, LLC. The offering includes three tranches of notes with varying interest rates and maturity dates, aiming to raise capital alongside existing funds. This move follows a strong stock performance for Howmet, with recent analyst upgrades and a planned dividend payment.

feed.title: DELL vs. SMCI: Which AI-Server Stock Offers Better Growth Opportunity?
feed.summary: This article compares Dell Technologies (DELL) and Super Micro Computer (SMCI) as AI-server investment opportunities, highlighting Dell's diversification and scale against Super Micro Computer's focused, high-growth AI strategy. While Dell faces margin pressures and cyclical exposure, Super Micro Computer's pure-play AI leverage, rapid revenue growth, and better valuation make it a more attractive investment, despite its own profitability challenges and revenue concentration. The article concludes that SMCI offers a better growth opportunity based on its AI focus and valuation.

feed.title: A Look at Ryerson Holding's Upcoming Earnings Report
feed.summary: Ryerson Holding (NYSE: RYI) is scheduled to release its quarterly earnings report on Thursday, February 19, 2026. Analysts predict an EPS of $-0.49 for the quarter. Investors will be looking for the company to surpass this estimate and provide positive guidance for future growth, as guidance often impacts stock prices more than past performance.

feed.title: Earnings Estimates Moving Higher for Applied Materials (AMAT): Time to Buy?
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) is showing solid improvement in earnings estimates, leading to strong short-term price momentum. Its Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) indicates growing analyst optimism on its earnings prospects, which historically correlates with near-term stock price movements. With shares already up 12.9% in the past four weeks and promising earnings growth, the article suggests AMAT could be a good addition to an investor's portfolio.

feed.title: Demystifying Welltower: Insights From 8 Analyst Reviews
feed.summary: Eight analysts have provided ratings on Welltower (NYSE: WELL) over the last three months, with an average price target of $226.62, marking a 7.04% increase from the previous average. The company shows a strong market capitalization, impressive revenue growth of 41.24% as of December 31, 2025, and an excellent net margin of 3.08%, though its Return on Equity (ROE) is below industry averages. Analyst actions, including updated ratings and price targets, indicate changing sentiments reflecting Welltower's market performance and financial health.

feed.title: Demystifying SLM: Insights From 9 Analyst Reviews
feed.summary: Over the past three months, nine analysts have provided diverse evaluations for SLM (NASDAQ: SLM), resulting in an average 12-month price target of $30.44, down 8.06% from previous targets. The company, which specializes in private education loans, shows strong profitability with a net margin of 50.51% and a high ROE of 10.69%, despite facing revenue growth challenges and a relatively smaller market capitalization. Analysts' individual ratings and price target adjustments reflect a dynamic perception of SLM's market position, emphasizing the importance of staying informed on these expert insights.

feed.title: Honeywell Aerospace appoints Brian Grace VP of communications
feed.summary: Honeywell Aerospace has appointed Brian Grace as its new VP of communications. Grace, who previously founded the advisory firm True North, returns to the aerospace industry after starting his career at a Honeywell spin-off. This appointment comes as Honeywell prepares to spin off Honeywell Aerospace into an independent publicly traded company, a transition expected to be completed in the third quarter of 2026.
